verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor may process customer data in any region where Vendor maintains infrastructure, including the United States, Ireland, and Singapore.
verdicts.note: Permits US and Singapore processing of EU data with only reasonable-efforts language, not a guarantee of EU residency.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may audit Vendor's information security controls once per calendar year, on not less than 60 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Sixty days' notice falls between the 30-day pass threshold and the 90-day violation threshold.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Termination for convenience is permitted by either party on 90 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Termination for convenience requires exactly 90 days' notice, within the 90-day maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor's aggregate liability under this Agreement is capped at fees paid by the customer in the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
verdicts.note: Cap is 12 months of fees; data breach is covered by the cap, and gross negligence data breaches carry unlimited liability.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor maintains a list of subprocessors at vendor.example/subprocessors. Vendor will update the list within 30 days of any change.
verdicts.note: Post-hoc list update within 30 days does not satisfy the requirement for at least 30 days' advance written notice before adding a subprocessor.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Vendor will notify the customer within 96 hours of confirming a security incident affecting the customer's data.
verdicts.note: 96-hour notification window exceeds the required 72-hour maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of California, United States.
verdicts.note: California has CCPA/CPRA data protection legislation but is not among the explicitly listed passing jurisdictions; treating as unclear pending legal review.

verdicts.evidence_quote: All customer data will be processed exclusively within the European Economic Area (EEA). Globex will not transfer customer data outside the EEA without the customer's prior written consent.
verdicts.note: Explicitly restricts all processing to the EEA with transfers outside only on prior written consent.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may request an audit of Globex's security controls on 30 days' written notice, not more than twice per calendar year.
verdicts.note: Thirty days' notice meets the policy's 30-day pass threshold exactly.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may terminate for convenience on 60 days' written notice.
verdicts.note: Sixty days' notice for convenience termination is within the 90-day maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex's aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. The cap does not apply to liability for data breach, gross negligence, or wilful misconduct.
verdicts.note: Cap is 12 months of fees and data breach liability is explicitly uncapped, exceeding the policy requirement.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex will provide the customer with at least 30 days' written notice before engaging any new subprocessor.
verdicts.note: At least 30 days' advance written notice before adding a new subprocessor meets the policy minimum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Globex will notify the customer within 24 hours of detecting a confirmed security incident affecting the customer's data.
verdicts.note: 24-hour notification window is well within the 72-hour maximum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales.
verdicts.note: England and Wales is an explicitly named passing jurisdiction with a mature data protection regime.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Customer data is stored in Sirius's data centres in Malaysia and Singapore. Sirius may relocate data between its facilities at its sole discretion for operational reasons.
verdicts.note: All data is stored in APAC (Malaysia and Singapore) with no EU residency provision whatsoever.

verdicts.evidence_quote: The customer may, on not less than 180 days' notice and not more than once every two years, conduct an audit of Sirius's security controls.
verdicts.note: 180 days' notice far exceeds the 90-day violation threshold.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Termination for convenience is not permitted during the initial term.
verdicts.note: Termination for convenience is explicitly prohibited during the initial term.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius's aggregate liability under this Agreement is capped at three (3) months of fees paid. This cap applies to all claims, including those arising from data breach or loss.
verdicts.note: Three-month liability cap is well below the required 12-month minimum.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius may engage any subprocessor it deems appropriate without prior notice to or consent from the customer.
verdicts.note: Vendor explicitly reserves the right to add subprocessors without any prior notice.

verdicts.evidence_quote: Sirius will notify the customer of confirmed security incidents in due course, taking into account the nature and circumstances of the incident.
verdicts.note: 'In due course' is not a specific notification window and does not satisfy the 72-hour maximum requirement.

verdicts.evidence_quote: This Agreement is governed by the laws of Malaysia.
verdicts.note: Malaysia is an explicitly named weak-enforcement jurisdiction under the policy.
